chunk_summary: Therapeutic feeding in SAM for infants under 6 months prioritizes breastfeeding restoration using SST/SNS, while older children require phased feeding with F-75 and F-100 formulas, micronutrient supplementation, and careful monitoring for complications.
learning_objectives: Understand therapeutic feeding protocols for infants under 6 months with SAM, including relactation strategies.
Learn phased feeding approaches for children with SAM, including the use of F-75 and F-100 formulas.
Recognize the importance of micronutrient supplementation and rehydration protocols in malnourished children.
Identify outpatient management options for uncomplicated SAM using RUTF.
key_facts: For infants under 6 months with SAM, prioritize breastfeeding restoration using SST or SNS.
F-75 formula is used during the stabilization phase to avoid refeeding complications; F-100 is used for rehabilitation.
Micronutrient supplementation includes vitamin A, zinc, and multivitamins, with iron delayed until stabilization.
ReSoMal is used for cautious rehydration in malnourished children to prevent complications.
Uncomplicated SAM with good appetite can be managed outpatient with RUTF.
reference_quote: For severe acute malnutrition, feeding is medical therapy and must follow phased physiology. (Source: WHO Pocket Book of Hospital Care for Children, 2nd Edition)
document_key_topics: Therapeutic feeding in SAM for infants under 6 months
Phased feeding with F-75 and F-100 formulas
